fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: West Virginia Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to West Virginia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity west virginia, how to transfer audiologist license to west virginia, audiologist compact west virginia, west virginia audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: West Virginia Board of Examiners for SLP and Audiology
board.mailing_address:
WV Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
950 Kanawha Blvd., E., Suite 300
Charleston, WV 25301

quick_facts.exam_name: Audiology Examination of the Praxis Series offered by the Educational Testing Service

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in West Virginia.

steps.title: Create an Account on the eLicense Portal
steps.description: Visit the West Virginia eLicense Portal (https://wvbeslpa.elicense.wv.gov/) and register for a new user account. You will need to provide an email address and create a password.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created account on the West Virginia eLicense Portal and select the application for Audiologist licensure. Fill out all required sections, including personal information, educational history, and details of your supervised professional experience.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Upload all necessary documentation directly through the West Virginia eLicense Portal. This typically includes official transcripts from your Master's or Doctoral program, verification of supervised professional experience (e.g., Clinical Fellowship Year), and official scores from the Praxis exam in Audiology.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Pay the initial application fee of $200 and any other associated fees as prompted through the secure payment system on the West Virginia eLicense Portal. This fee is required to process your application.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Approval
steps.description: Once your complete application and all supporting documents are received, the West Virginia Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology will review your submission. You will be notified via the portal or email regarding the status and final approval of your license.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a West Virginia audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does West Virginia have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. West Virginia participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in West Virginia without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to West Virginia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in West Virginia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in West Virginia

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the West Virginia eLicense Portal, click 'Register Now' to create a new user account with your email address and password. Once registered, log in to access the application options and apply for a new license.
